title: Mechanical Thrombectomy vs Thrombolysis in Large Vessel Occlusion Stroke
research_question: In adults with acute ischemic stroke due to anterior circulation large vessel occlusion, does mechanical endovascular thrombectomy using stent retrievers improve 90-day functional independence compared to intravenous alteplase alone?

pico.population: adults presenting within 6 hours of symptom onset with acute ischemic stroke due to anterior circulation large vessel occlusion (LVO)
pico.intervention: mechanical endovascular thrombectomy using modern stent retrievers or direct aspiration catheters
pico.comparator: standard medical therapy utilizing intravenous alteplase (tPA) or tenecteplase alone
pico.outcome: functional independence at 90 days defined by a modified Rankin Scale (mRS) score of 0 to 2, and rates of symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage

inclusion_criteria.text: Study must include patients with confirmed anterior circulation LVO.
Intervention must involve mechanical clot retrieval.

exclusion_criteria.text: Studies using outdated first-generation devices like the MERCI retriever.
